title: calacinum peruvianum
intro: Calacinum peruvianum, also known as Peruvian mint or Lemon mint, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is native to South America, particularly in the Andes region of Peru, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of calacinum peruvianum include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems such as coughs and colds. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for use in herbal remedies.
